aot_build_blocker_finding: `use "self/stdlib/<module>"` is NOT followed by hexa_v2 codegen during `hexa build` AOT path. flatten_imports.hexa exists at tool/ but the build wrapper does not invoke it. Workaround: inline external-use symbols into a /tmp/ source copy. Production fix would be to make hexa_v2 walk `use` declarations or to invoke flatten_imports as preprocess. Recorded as F-AOT-BUILD-1 candidate.

finding: A4 structural sub-tree dedup yields 0% saving on every production-class corpus measured (text_heavy / struct_audit / small_file / mixed_real). A12 column-prefix yields 0% saving on every production corpus. A15 nested-subschema yields 0% saving on every production corpus.
explanation: A4/A12/A15 are SCHEMA-DENSE-CORPUS optimizers — they fire on multi-row JSONL where (a) sub-trees repeat ≥A4_MIN_FREQ times (A4), (b) row column prefixes share ≥MIN_PREFIX bytes across MIN_ROWS=16 rows (A12), or (c) nested arrays-of-objects share subschema ≥MIN_ROWS=4 (A15). Production HXC corpus mix in the 4-class sample does NOT exhibit these per-row schema-canonical signatures at byte-mass — the corpora are predominantly heterogeneous JSONL with varying schemas + log-line content. A18 v2 LZ-PPM order-4 already captures the byte-level repetition that A4/A12/A15 would have found at structural level.
selftest_evidence: All three modules selftest PASS on synthetic fixtures (atlas_like 38%, mixed 33%, deep_nested 20% for A4; bytes 1191->760 for A15; 722->518 for A12) — algorithms ARE correct on the corpus profile they target.
cross-link: matches a45778db A20 production reality (4/5 corpora 0% gate REJECT). Same root cause family: schema-canonical optimizers REJECT high-entropy heterogeneous production JSONL. Generalized falsifier: F-A4-PRODUCTION-1 / F-A12-PRODUCTION-1 / F-A15-PRODUCTION-1 (corpus-class-conditional, NOT_RETIRED, NOT_TRIPPED, eligibility behavior correct).

generalization_of_C8_finding: NOT generalized to cohort 2. C8 prepass-hurts pattern is SPECIFIC to entropy-coder-prepasses (A17 PPM order-3 emits already-rank-coded byte stream that defeats LZ-tokenization). Structural prepasses (A4/A12/A15) do NOT emit entropy-coded byte streams; they emit text with structural ref-tokens. A18 LZ-PPM order-4 sees structurally-modified input as similar entropy to original input → null harm.
mutually_exclusive_chains_confirmed: A20 + A18 on production corpora (a45778db D4 — gate REJECT)
matrix_verdict: structural-prepass + entropy-coder = WELL-STACK pattern (no harm); entropy-prepass + entropy-coder = HURTFUL-STACK pattern (C8). Cohort 2 chains are well-stack but ZERO-LIFT on production corpus mix — corpus eligibility gate dominates.

hexa_v2 codegen does NOT follow `use "self/stdlib/<module>"` imports during AOT build. Symptom: `Undefined symbol _<exported_fn>` at clang link. Affects A4/A7/A8/A10 (all use argv_skip). Workaround: inline import target into module copy. Production fix: hexa_v2 walk `use` graph or auto-invoke flatten_imports preprocess. Captured for hexa-lang infrastructure backlog.
